basics.name: Samarth Bhadane
basics.label: Software Engineer
basics.summary: A Master's Student at University of California, San Diego

work.name: UC San Diego
highlights: Responsible for grading assignments and exams as well as Conducting weekly discussion sessions and office hours, guiding students through complex cryptographic protocols and implementations

work.name: UC San Diego
highlights: Worked on a research project under guidance of Prof. Nadia Heninger focused on identifying RSA keys with biased prime numbers generated from random number generators, inspired by real-world cases.

Established a comprehensive repository of RSA public keys by aggregating data from sources including certificate transparency logs, internet scans, and GitHub keys, ensuring the repository's adaptability for future projects.
Implemented an attack based on a relevant research paper to detect and exploit biases in RSA moduli, targeting keys with significant bias towards 0 bits in both prime factors.

work.name: SolUltima Technologies Pvt. Ltd.
highlights: Responsible for development of secure Smart Contracts compatible with Blockchains based on Ethereum Virtual Machine
Developed the Smart Contracts required for a Staking Platform for AVXL token Holders with Algorithm to earn rewards on User’s Stake using Solidity.
Deployed the contract successfully on BSC Smart Chain and users staked more than 5 million USD worth of AVXL tokens.

work.name: Zubi Infotech Pvt. Ltd.
highlights: Developed a secure Hardware Wallet Application for XinFin Network on Ledger devices, ensuring the protection of digital assets, while working at a startup with more than 50 employees.
Collaborated with a 5-person team to design the wallet app and intuitive UI/UX interface, delivering consistent updates to stakeholders.
Built the app in C/C++ and used JavaScript for the companion app to enable communication with the network.
Wrote comprehensive documentation and a technical blog post to share knowledge within the company and tech community.

work.name: PICT Computational Linguistics Lab
highlights: Conducted in-depth research on image dehazing techniques, exploring various methods to remove haze from images.
Implemented and analyzed research papers focusing on diverse algorithms, including prior-based methods like DCP and Neural Network-based solutions for depth estimation and haze removal.
Contributed to a paper, detailing findings and insights from the research, showcasing a strong grasp of the subject matter and analytical abilities.
